messages.content:
You are a provider knowledge node in a distributed consensus network. You are not a dedicated summarizer or dedicated reviewer role; you are a domain provider that performs answer, synthesis, and grading using your domain knowledge.
Global rules:
- Output must be one valid JSON object only; no markdown.
- Use UNKNOWN when uncertain; do not guess.
- FAIL only on clear, concrete errors or contradictions.
- Do not invent point_id values; only use IDs provided by client.
- Be concise and technically precise.
- When grading, do not penalize missing info; only flag clear errors.

Domain: coding_debugging.
Scope: Python/JavaScript backend, debugging workflow, error handling, idempotency patterns, retry/backoff implementation basics.
Working style: pragmatic bug-fixer; concrete steps; minimal fluff; edge-case focused.

TASK: ANSWER
Return EXACT JSON schema:
{
  "abstain": true|false,
  "points": [{"text": "string"}]
}
Rules:
- Return ONLY high-confidence points inside your declared domain scope.
- If the query is mostly outside your scope, set abstain=true and return {"points": []}.
- If abstain=true, points must be empty.
- Each point must be atomic and <= 160 chars.
- Do not add generic cross-domain filler.
Input JSON:
{"query": "We use WebSockets for real-time updates. Clients reconnect often and sometimes receive duplicate or out-of-order events. We also worry about replayed tokens. How should we design ordering/deduplication and secure the channel?", "input_text": ""}

raw_content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event on the server."},{"text":"Clients track the last sequence received and ignore any event with a lower or equal number."},{"text":"Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache (e.g., an LRU set) keyed by event ID to drop repeats."},{"text":"Use idempotent handlers so processing the same event twice has no side effects."},{"text":"Secure tokens with a nonce or timestamp and validate them on each reconnect; reject reused nonces."},{"text":"Run WebSocket over TLS (wss://) and optionally sign messages with HMAC to detect tampering."}]}

message.content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event on the server."},{"text":"Clients track the last sequence received and ignore any event with a lower or equal number."},{"text":"Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache (e.g., an LRU set) keyed by event ID to drop repeats."},{"text":"Use idempotent handlers so processing the same event twice has no side effects."},{"text":"Secure tokens with a nonce or timestamp and validate them on each reconnect; reject reused nonces."},{"text":"Run WebSocket over TLS (wss://) and optionally sign messages with HMAC to detect tampering."}]}

raw_content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign each event a strictly increasing sequence number per client session; clients drop events with numbers ≤ last processed."},{"text":"Include the sequence number in the WebSocket message payload and have the client acknowledge receipt to detect gaps."},{"text":"On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number."},{"text":"Use TLS (wss://) to encrypt the channel and protect tokens from eavesdropping."},{"text":"Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client’s session; validate on each reconnect."},{"text":"Add a nonce or timestamp to each token and reject any token older than a configurable window to prevent replay."},{"text":"Maintain a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by token or event ID for a limited time to drop duplicates."},{"text":"Enable WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection."}]}

message.content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign each event a strictly increasing sequence number per client session; clients drop events with numbers ≤ last processed."},{"text":"Include the sequence number in the WebSocket message payload and have the client acknowledge receipt to detect gaps."},{"text":"On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number."},{"text":"Use TLS (wss://) to encrypt the channel and protect tokens from eavesdropping."},{"text":"Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client’s session; validate on each reconnect."},{"text":"Add a nonce or timestamp to each token and reject any token older than a configurable window to prevent replay."},{"text":"Maintain a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by token or event ID for a limited time to drop duplicates."},{"text":"Enable WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection."}]}

raw_content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Use TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic to ensure confidentiality and integrity of messages."},{"text":"Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event; server discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages."},{"text":"Include a per‑connection nonce (e.g., JWT jti) and reject any message with a reused nonce to prevent replay."},{"text":"Issue short‑lived signed tokens (JWT) bound to the connection ID; verify signature, expiration, and audience on each reconnect."},{"text":"Store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache with TTL and reject any token seen before to enforce one‑time use."},{"text":"On client reconnect, require a fresh token; invalidate the previous token to stop replay of stale credentials."},{"text":"Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can resend missed events and the server can detect gaps."}]}

message.content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Use TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic to ensure confidentiality and integrity of messages."},{"text":"Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event; server discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages."},{"text":"Include a per‑connection nonce (e.g., JWT jti) and reject any message with a reused nonce to prevent replay."},{"text":"Issue short‑lived signed tokens (JWT) bound to the connection ID; verify signature, expiration, and audience on each reconnect."},{"text":"Store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache with TTL and reject any token seen before to enforce one‑time use."},{"text":"On client reconnect, require a fresh token; invalidate the previous token to stop replay of stale credentials."},{"text":"Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can resend missed events and the server can detect gaps."}]}

raw_content: {"synthesis_text":"Secure the WebSocket channel with TLS (wss://) and optionally sign each message with an HMAC to detect tampering. Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to every event on the server and include it in the payload; clients keep the last sequence they processed and ignore any event with a lower or equal number. The server also discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages based on the sequence. Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache keyed by event ID to drop repeats that may arrive after reconnection. Implement idempotent handlers so that accidental re‑processing has no side effects. On reconnect the client sends its last processed sequence number, allowing the server to resend any missed events. Require a fresh short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) bound to the connection ID for each reconnect and invalidate the previous token; include a per‑connection nonce (jti) and reject any reused nonce to prevent replay attacks.","used_points":["N1:5:698fc4b2","N1:0:5f087a3d","N1:1:db770d6b","N5:1:b8de628a","N8:6:1b0068fe","N1:3:18b857db","N8:2:2a55e671","N5:5:4509bbb5","N5:3:b4db48aa","N5:2:a5fdc5be"]}

The server also discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages based on the sequence. Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache keyed by event ID to drop repeats that may arrive after reconnection. Implement idempotent handlers so that accidental re‑processing has no side effects. On reconnect the client sends its last processed sequence number, allowing the server to resend any missed events. Require a fresh short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) bound to the connection ID for each reconnect and invalidate the previous token; include a per‑connection nonce (jti) and reject any reused nonce to prevent replay attacks.","used_points":["N1:5:698fc4b2","N1:0:5f087a3d","N1:1:db770d6b","N5:1:b8de628a","N8:6:1b0068fe","N1:3:18b857db","N8:2:2a55e671","N5:5:4509bbb5","N5:3:b4db48aa","N5:2:a5fdc5be"]}

{
  "synthesis_text": "Secure the WebSocket channel with TLS (wss://) to protect tokens and message integrity (N8:3:9a4f1bd4). Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client session and store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache to enforce one‑time use, rejecting reused tokens (N8:4:6aced31c, N5:4:b8a1d3d5). Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event (N1:0:5f087a3d) and have clients track the last sequence they processed, discarding any event with a lower or equal number (N1:1:db770d6b). On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number (N8:2:2a55e671). Maintain a short‑term server‑side deduplication cache keyed by event ID to drop duplicate messages that may arrive due to reconnection or network reordering (N8:6:1b0068fe). Use WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection (N8:7:c7fa53d0). Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can acknowledge received events and request retransmission of gaps (N5:6:9083ce5a). Finally, design event handlers to be idempotent so that any accidental duplicate processing has no side effects (N1:3:18b857db).",

the server resends any missed events starting from the next number.", "N8:3:9a4f1bd4": "Use TLS (wss://) to encrypt the channel and protect tokens from eavesdropping.", "N8:4:6aced31c": "Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client’s session; messages.content:
You are a provider knowledge node in a distributed consensus network. You are not a dedicated summarizer or dedicated reviewer role; you are a domain provider that performs answer, synthesis, and grading using your domain knowledge.
Global rules:
- Output must be one valid JSON object only; no markdown.
- Use UNKNOWN when uncertain; do not guess.
- FAIL only on clear, concrete errors or contradictions.
- Do not invent point_id values; only use IDs provided by client.
- Be concise and technically precise.
- When grading, do not penalize missing info; only flag clear errors.

Domain: security_defensive.
Scope: Auth/session safety, JWT vs cookies, token storage risks, CSRF/XSS basics, secure coding practices for web backends.
Working style: defensive security expert; practical mitigations; conservative judgments.

TASK: GRADE_BATCH
Return EXACT JSON schema:
{
  "votes": [
    {
      "target_id": "node_id",
      "valid": "PASS|FAIL|UNKNOWN",
      "agree_points": ["point_id"],
      "reject_points": ["point_id"],
      "unknown_points": ["point_id"],
      "note": "string"
    }
  ]
}
Rules:
- Include exactly one vote per target in targets list.
- FAIL only on clear errors/contradictions.
- UNKNOWN if insufficient information or outside your scope.
- Only classify IDs present in each target_used_points.
- For each target, every target_used_points ID must appear in exactly one list.
Input JSON:
{"query": "We use WebSockets for real-time updates. Clients reconnect often and sometimes receive duplicate or out-of-order events. We also worry about replayed tokens. How should we design ordering/deduplication and secure the channel?", "input_text": "", "targets": [{"target_id": "N1", "target_draft": "Secure the WebSocket channel with TLS (wss://) and optionally sign each message with an HMAC to detect tampering. Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to every event on the server and include it in the payload; clients keep the last sequence they processed and ignore any event with a lower or equal number. The server also discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages based on the sequence. Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache keyed by event ID to drop repeats that may arrive after reconnection. Implement idempotent handlers so that accidental re‑processing has no side effects. On reconnect the client sends its last processed sequence number, allowing the server to resend any missed events. Require a fresh short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) bound to the connection ID for each reconnect and invalidate the previous token; include a per‑connection nonce (jti) and reject any reused nonce to prevent replay attacks.", "target_used_points": ["N1:5:698fc4b2", "N1:0:5f087a3d", "N1:1:db770d6b", "N5:1:b8de628a", "N8:6:1b0068fe", "N1:3:18b857db", "N8:2:2a55e671", "N5:5:4509bbb5", "N5:3:b4db48aa", "N5:2:a5fdc5be"]}, {"target_id": "N8", "target_draft": "Secure the WebSocket channel with TLS (wss://) to protect tokens and message integrity (N8:3:9a4f1bd4). Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client session and store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache to enforce one‑time use, rejecting reused tokens (N8:4:6aced31c, N5:4:b8a1d3d5). Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event (N1:0:5f087a3d) and have clients track the last sequence they processed, discarding any event with a lower or equal number (N1:1:db770d6b). On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number (N8:2:2a55e671). Maintain a short‑term server‑side deduplication cache keyed by event ID to drop duplicate messages that may arrive due to reconnection or network reordering (N8:6:1b0068fe). Use WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection (N8:7:c7fa53d0). Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can acknowledge received events and request retransmission of gaps (N5:6:9083ce5a). Finally, design event handlers to be idempotent so that any accidental duplicate processing has no side effects (N1:3:18b857db).", "target_used_points": ["N8:3:9a4f1bd4", "N8:4:6aced31c", "N5:4:b8a1d3d5", "N1:0:5f087a3d", "N1:1:db770d6b", "N8:2:2a55e671", "N8:6:1b0068fe", "N8:7:c7fa53d0", "N5:6:9083ce5a", "N1:3:18b857db"]}], "points_map": {"N1:0:5f087a3d": "Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event on the server.", "N1:1:db770d6b": "Clients track the last sequence received and ignore any event with a lower or equal number.", "N1:2:6cd7ec92": "Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache (e.g., an LRU set) keyed by event ID to drop repeats.", "N1:3:18b857db": "Use idempotent handlers so processing the same event twice has no side effects.", "N1:4:23d43456": "Secure tokens with a nonce or timestamp and validate them on each reconnect; reject reused nonces.", "N1:5:698fc4b2": "Run WebSocket over TLS (wss://) and optionally sign messages with HMAC to detect tampering.", "N8:0:3177e4db": "Assign each event a strictly increasing sequence number per client session; clients drop events with numbers ≤ last processed.", "N8:1:a1ea52e6": "Include the sequence number in the WebSocket message payload and have the client acknowledge receipt to detect gaps.", "N8:2:2a55e671": "On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number.", "N8:3:9a4f1bd4": "Use TLS (wss://) to encrypt the channel and protect tokens from eavesdropping.", "N8:4:6aced31c": "Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client’s session; validate on each reconnect.", "N8:5:41690530": "Add a nonce or timestamp to each token and reject any token older than a configurable window to prevent replay.", "N8:6:1b0068fe": "Maintain a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by token or event ID for a limited time to drop duplicates.", "N8:7:c7fa53d0": "Enable WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection.", "N5:0:e69b5017": "Use TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic to ensure confidentiality and integrity of messages.", "N5:1:b8de628a": "Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event; server discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages.", "N5:2:a5fdc5be": "Include a per‑connection nonce (e.g., JWT jti) and reject any message with a reused nonce to prevent replay.", "N5:3:b4db48aa": "Issue short‑lived signed tokens (JWT) bound to the connection ID; verify signature, expiration, and audience on each reconnect.", "N5:4:b8a1d3d5": "Store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache with TTL and reject any token seen before to enforce one‑time use.", "N5:5:4509bbb5": "On client reconnect, require a fresh token; invalidate the previous token to stop replay of stale credentials.", "N5:6:9083ce5a": "Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can resend missed events and the server can detect gaps."}}
